name: Perfect Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Half-and-half; about 3/4 cup Butter or margarine
recipeInstructions:
Combine cocoa and boiling water, stirring until smooth. Set aside to cool.
Combine butter, sugar, eggs, and vanilla in an electric mixer set to high speed. Beat for 5 minutes until light and fluffy.
In a separate bowl, combine dry ingredients. Add the cocoa mixture alternately with the dry mixture, beginning and ending with the flour mixture. Do not overbeat. Pour batter into three greased and floured 9-inch round cake pans.
Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 25 to 30 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool in pans for 10 minutes before removing and allowing layers to cool completely.
Spread filling between layers, then frost over the top and sides of the cake. Garnish with pecans if desired. Serve immediately or refrigerate until ready to serve.
Filling:
Beat whipping cream and vanilla until foamy. Gradually add confectioners' sugar, beating until peaks hold their shape. Chill.
Frosting:
Combine chocolate morsels, half-and-half, and butter in a saucepan. Cook over medium heat until the chocolate melts. Remove from heat and add confectioners' sugar, mixing well. Set the saucepan in ice and beat until the frosting holds its shape and loses its gloss. Add a few more drops of half-and-half if needed to achieve spreading consistency.
